event: Brewster Kahle founds Internet Archive in San Francisco (May 10)
Web crawling begins; first archived pages saved
Alexa Internet co-founded by Kahle (sister project)
Alexa Internet sold to Amazon for $250M in stock
Internet Archive begins receiving Alexa's web crawl data
Wayback Machine launched publicly (October) at UC Berkeley
First Form 990 on public record (FY2002 990-PF)
Archive-It web archiving subscription service launched (late 2005)
Open Library founded by Aaron Swartz / Brewster Kahle / Alexis Rossi
Open Content Alliance forms (multi-library partnership)
300 Funston Avenue (former Fourth Church of Christ Scientist) purchased for $4.5M
Move-in to Funston building; main sanctuary becomes primary workspace
TV News Search & Borrow service launched
IA classified as 501(c)(3) public charity (transition from private foundation 990-PF to 990 begins)
$600K fire at one of the IA scanning centers (one of approx 30 worldwide at the time)
Aaron Swartz dies; Open Library co-founder
IRS reclassifies IA as 501(c)(3) public charity (current ruling date)
The Great 78 Project formally launched (78rpm digitization w/ ARChive of Contemporary Music + George Blood Audio)
Mellon Long Tail journal preservation R&D grant (24mo)
FY2019 revenue spikes to $36.7M (largest year on record through 2024)
National Emergency Library opens (March 24) -- pandemic response; CDL one-to-one ratio suspended
Hachette / Penguin Random House / HarperCollins / Wiley file copyright suit
National Emergency Library closes
Mellon $1.13M grant for Community Webs nationwide public library web archives network
Internet Archive Scholar launched
District court rules for publishers in Hachette v. Internet Archive
UMG / Sony / Concord file Great 78 Project copyright suit (initially $621M; later $412M)
Mellon $750K grant for final phase of Community Webs
FY2023 reports $32.7M expenses against $23.7M revenue: legal/judgment costs surface
DDoS attacks attributed to SN_BLACKMETA group begin
2nd Circuit affirms Hachette ruling against IA
Major data breach: ~31 million user accounts exposed via leaked GitLab auth tokens
Second breach: Zendesk support email system compromised
Public site begins partial restoration
IA announces it will not seek Supreme Court review of Hachette decision
UMG v. Internet Archive (Great 78) settled; confidential resolution
Wayback Machine reaches 1 trillion archived web pages
Internet Archive turns 30
